SELECT 
    type,
    COUNT(*) as count,
    ROUND(COUNT(*) * 100.0 / SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER(), 2) as percentage
FROM fraud_ts
GROUP BY type
ORDER BY count DESC;

In [0]:
%sql
SELECT 
    SUM(isFraud) as total_fraud_cases,
    SUM(isFlaggedFraud) as total_flagged_cases,
    ROUND(SUM(isFraud) * 100.0 / COUNT(*), 4) as fraud_rate_percentage,
    ROUND(AVG(amount), 2) as avg_transaction_amount,
    MIN(amount) as min_amount,
    MAX(amount) as max_amount
FROM fraud_ts;
total_fraud_casestotal_flagged_casesfraud_rate_percentageavg_transaction_amountmin_amountmax_amount

Cleaning Data¶

In [0]:
%sql
CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE clean_transactions AS
SELECT 
    step as time_step,
    type as transaction_type,
    amount,
    nameOrig as customer_id,
    nameDest as recipient_id,
    oldbalanceOrg as customer_old_balance,
    newbalanceOrig as customer_new_balance,
    oldbalanceDest as recipient_old_balance,
    newbalanceDest as recipient_new_balance,
    isFraud,
    isFlaggedFraud,
    CASE 
        WHEN nameDest LIKE 'M%' THEN 'Merchant'
        ELSE 'Customer'
    END as recipient_type,
    CASE 
        WHEN amount < 10000 THEN 'Small (<10K)'
        WHEN amount BETWEEN 10000 AND 100000 THEN 'Medium (10K-100K)'
        WHEN amount BETWEEN 100001 AND 500000 THEN 'Large (100K-500K)'
        ELSE 'Very Large (>500K)'
    END as amount_category,
    CASE WHEN amount <= 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END as flag_invalid_amount
FROM fraud_ts
WHERE amount > 0;
num_affected_rowsnum_inserted_rows

In [0]:
%sql
CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE clean_transactions AS
SELECT 
    *,
    -- Time calculations (assuming 1 step = 1 hour)
    FLOOR(time_step / 24) + 1 as day_number,
    time_step % 24 as hour_of_day,
    -- Time period name (for easy reporting)
    CASE 
        WHEN time_step % 24 BETWEEN 0 AND 5 THEN 'Late Night (00-05)'
        WHEN time_step % 24 BETWEEN 6 AND 11 THEN 'Morning (06-11)'
        WHEN time_step % 24 BETWEEN 12 AND 17 THEN 'Afternoon (12-17)'
        ELSE 'Evening (18-23)'
    END as time_period,
    -- Day of week (fraud often higher on weekends)
    CASE 
        WHEN day_number % 7 = 1 THEN 'Monday'
        WHEN day_number % 7 = 2 THEN 'Tuesday'
        WHEN day_number % 7 = 3 THEN 'Wednesday'
        WHEN day_number % 7 = 4 THEN 'Thursday'
        WHEN day_number % 7 = 5 THEN 'Friday'
        WHEN day_number % 7 = 6 THEN 'Saturday'
        ELSE 'Sunday'
    END as day_of_week
FROM clean_transactions;
num_affected_rowsnum_inserted_rows

Basic Fraud Flags¶

In [0]:
%sql
CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE clean_transactions AS
SELECT 
    *,
    
    -- FLAG 1: Large transfers (over 500,000) - common in fraud
    CASE 
        WHEN transaction_type = 'TRANSFER' AND amount > 500000 THEN 1 
        ELSE 0 
    END as flag_large_transfer,
    
    -- FLAG 2: No balance change (possible fake transaction)
    -- If money leaves but balance doesn't change, suspicious!
    CASE 
        WHEN customer_old_balance = customer_new_balance 
             AND amount > 0 
             AND transaction_type IN ('TRANSFER', 'CASH_OUT') 
        THEN 1 
        ELSE 0 
    END as flag_no_balance_change,
    
    -- FLAG 3: Destination balance mismatch
    -- If math doesn't add up, data might be fabricated
    CASE 
        WHEN (recipient_old_balance + amount) != recipient_new_balance 
             AND recipient_new_balance IS NOT NULL 
        THEN 1 
        ELSE 0 
    END as flag_balance_mismatch,
    
    -- FLAG 4: Zero balance after transaction (possible money mule)
    CASE 
        WHEN customer_new_balance = 0 
             AND amount > 0 
        THEN 1 
        ELSE 0 
    END as flag_zero_after_txn
    
FROM clean_transactions;
num_affected_rowsnum_inserted_rows

Benford's Law Analysis¶

In [0]:
%sql
CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE clean_transactions AS
SELECT *,
CAST(SUBSTRING(CAST(amount AS STRING), 1, 1) AS INT) as amount_first_digit,
CASE WHEN amount > 1000 THEN
CASE WHEN CAST(SUBSTRING(CAST(amount AS STRING), 1, 1) AS INT) IN (7, 8, 9) 
THEN 1 
ELSE 0 
END
ELSE 0
END as flag_benford_unusual_digit
    
FROM clean_transactions;
num_affected_rowsnum_inserted_rows

This query shows if fraud follows natural patterns¶

In [0]:
%sql
SELECT amount_first_digit,
    COUNT(*) as total_txns,
    ROUND(COUNT(*) * 100.0 / SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER(), 2) as actual_percentage,
    SUM(isFraud) as fraud_cases,
    ROUND(SUM(isFraud) * 100.0 / COUNT(*), 2) as fraud_rate,

 -- Compare with Benford's expected
 
    CASE 
    WHEN amount_first_digit = 1 THEN 30.1
    WHEN amount_first_digit = 2 THEN 17.6
    WHEN amount_first_digit = 3 THEN 12.5
    WHEN amount_first_digit = 4 THEN 9.7
    WHEN amount_first_digit = 5 THEN 7.9
    WHEN amount_first_digit = 6 THEN 6.7
    WHEN amount_first_digit = 7 THEN 5.8
    WHEN amount_first_digit = 8 THEN 5.1
    WHEN amount_first_digit = 9 THEN 4.6
    END as benford_expected
FROM clean_transactions
WHERE amount > 1000
GROUP BY amount_first_digit
ORDER BY amount_first_digit;
amount_first_digittotal_txnsactual_percentagefraud_casesfraud_ratebenford_expected
1212170634.1125660.1230.1
2128641720.6814260.1117.6
378262512.589820.1312.5
45107668.217810.159.7
53800176.115860.157.9
63184475.125080.166.7
72899404.664930.175.8
82716744.374190.155.1
92583824.153940.154.6
In [0]:
%sql
CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE customer_history AS
SELECT 
    customer_id,
    COUNT(*) as total_customer_txns,
    AVG(amount) as avg_customer_amount,
    MAX(amount) as max_customer_amount,
    SUM(isFraud) as customer_fraud_count,
    -- Calculate customer's own Benford pattern
    SUM(CASE WHEN amount_first_digit IN (7,8,9) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 1.0 / COUNT(*) as unusual_digit_ratio
FROM clean_transactions
GROUP BY customer_id;
num_affected_rowsnum_inserted_rows
In [0]:
%sql
CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE clean_transactions AS
SELECT 
    t.*,
    h.total_customer_txns,
    h.avg_customer_amount,
    h.unusual_digit_ratio,
    
    -- ENHANCED FLAG 1: Benford + Time (3x weight for night transactions)
    CASE 
        WHEN t.amount > 1000 
             AND t.amount_first_digit IN (7, 8, 9)
             AND t.time_period IN ('Late Night (00-05)', 'Evening (18-23)')
             AND h.total_customer_txns > 5
        THEN 3  -- High suspicion
        WHEN t.amount > 1000 
             AND t.amount_first_digit IN (7, 8, 9)
        THEN 1  -- Medium suspicion
        ELSE 0
    END as flag_benford_time,

    -- ENHANCED FLAG 2: Pattern of unusual digits
    CASE 
        WHEN h.unusual_digit_ratio > 0.3  -- Customer uses unusual digits >30% of time
             AND t.amount_first_digit IN (7, 8, 9)
             AND h.total_customer_txns > 10
        THEN 3
        WHEN t.amount_first_digit IN (7, 8, 9)
             AND h.total_customer_txns > 10
             AND h.unusual_digit_ratio > 0.2
        THEN 2
        ELSE 0
    END as flag_benford_pattern,

    -- ENHANCED FLAG 3: Amount vs customer history
    CASE 
        WHEN t.amount > h.avg_customer_amount * 5  -- 5x normal
             AND t.amount_first_digit IN (7, 8, 9)
             AND h.total_customer_txns > 10
        THEN 3
        WHEN t.amount > h.avg_customer_amount * 3
             AND t.amount_first_digit IN (7, 8, 9)
        THEN 2
        ELSE 0
    END as flag_benford_amount_anomaly
    FROM clean_transactions t
LEFT JOIN customer_history h ON t.customer_id = h.customer_id;
num_affected_rowsnum_inserted_rows

Showing the first 8713 rows.

Velocity & Pattern Analysis¶

In [0]:
%sql
CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE clean_transactions AS
SELECT 
    *,
    
    -- Count of customer's transactions in last 5 steps (velocity)
    (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM clean_transactions t2 
     WHERE t2.customer_id = t.customer_id 
     AND t2.time_step BETWEEN t.time_step - 5 AND t.time_step) as recent_txn_count,
    
    -- Count of unusual digits in last 5 transactions
    (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM clean_transactions t2 
     WHERE t2.customer_id = t.customer_id 
     AND t2.amount_first_digit IN (7,8,9)
     AND t2.time_step BETWEEN t.time_step - 5 AND t.time_step) as recent_unusual_digits,
    
    -- VELOCITY FLAG: Too many transactions too quickly
    CASE 
        WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM clean_transactions t2 
              WHERE t2.customer_id = t.customer_id 
              AND t2.time_step BETWEEN t.time_step - 5 AND t.time_step) > 10
        THEN 2
        WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM clean_transactions t2 
              WHERE t2.customer_id = t.customer_id 
              AND t2.time_step BETWEEN t.time_step - 5 AND t.time_step) > 5
        THEN 1
        ELSE 0
    END as flag_high_velocity,
    
    -- PATTERN FLAG: Multiple unusual digits in short time
    CASE 
        WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM clean_transactions t2 
              WHERE t2.customer_id = t.customer_id 
              AND t2.amount_first_digit IN (7,8,9)
              AND t2.time_step BETWEEN t.time_step - 10 AND t.time_step) >= 3
        THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END as flag_unusual_pattern
    
FROM clean_transactions t;
num_affected_rowsnum_inserted_rows

Recipient Risk Analysis¶

In [0]:
%sql
CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE recipient_analysis AS
SELECT 
    recipient_id,
    recipient_type,
    COUNT(*) as times_received,
    COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id) as unique_senders,
    SUM(amount) as total_received,
    SUM(isFraud) as fraud_received,
    ROUND(SUM(isFraud) * 100.0 / COUNT(*), 2) as fraud_percentage,
    
    -- Recipient risk categories
    CASE 
        WHEN SUM(isFraud) > 5 THEN 'CRITICAL_RISK'
        WHEN SUM(isFraud) > 0 THEN 'FRAUD_EXPOSED'
        WHEN COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id) > 20 THEN 'HIGH_VOLUME'
        ELSE 'NORMAL'
    END as recipient_risk_category
    
FROM clean_transactions
GROUP BY recipient_id, recipient_type;

-- 8.2: Add recipient risk to transactions
CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE clean_transactions AS
SELECT 
    t.*,
    r.recipient_risk_category,
    CASE 
        WHEN r.fraud_percentage > 10 THEN 3
        WHEN r.fraud_percentage > 0 THEN 1
        ELSE 0
    END as flag_risky_recipient
    
FROM clean_transactions t
LEFT JOIN recipient_analysis r ON t.recipient_id = r.recipient_id;
num_affected_rowsnum_inserted_rows

Combined Risk Score¶

In [0]:
%sql
CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE clean_transactions AS
SELECT 
    *,
    
    -- Total risk score (sum of all weighted flags)
    (flag_large_transfer * 2 +
     flag_no_balance_change * 3 +
     flag_balance_mismatch * 3 +
     flag_zero_after_txn * 1 +
     flag_benford_time * 2 +
     flag_benford_pattern * 3 +
     flag_benford_amount_anomaly * 3 +
     flag_high_velocity * 2 +
     flag_unusual_pattern * 4 +
     flag_risky_recipient * 3 +
     isFlaggedFraud * 2) as total_risk_score,
     
    -- Risk level based on score
    CASE 
        WHEN (flag_large_transfer * 2 +
              flag_no_balance_change * 3 +
              flag_balance_mismatch * 3 +
              flag_zero_after_txn * 1 +
              flag_benford_time * 2 +
              flag_benford_pattern * 3 +
              flag_benford_amount_anomaly * 3 +
              flag_high_velocity * 2 +
              flag_unusual_pattern * 4 +
              flag_risky_recipient * 3 +
              isFlaggedFraud * 2) >= 10
        THEN 'CRITICAL_RISK'
        WHEN (flag_large_transfer * 2 +
              flag_no_balance_change * 3 +
              flag_balance_mismatch * 3 +
              flag_zero_after_txn * 1 +
              flag_benford_time * 2 +
              flag_benford_pattern * 3 +
              flag_benford_amount_anomaly * 3 +
              flag_high_velocity * 2 +
              flag_unusual_pattern * 4 +
              flag_risky_recipient * 3 +
              isFlaggedFraud * 2) >= 5
        THEN 'HIGH_RISK'
        WHEN (flag_large_transfer * 2 +
              flag_no_balance_change * 3 +
              flag_balance_mismatch * 3 +
              flag_zero_after_txn * 1 +
              flag_benford_time * 2 +
              flag_benford_pattern * 3 +
              flag_benford_amount_anomaly * 3 +
              flag_high_velocity * 2 +
              flag_unusual_pattern * 4 +
              flag_risky_recipient * 3 +
              isFlaggedFraud * 2) >= 2
        THEN 'MEDIUM_RISK'
        ELSE 'LOW_RISK'
    END as risk_level
    
FROM clean_transactions;
num_affected_rowsnum_inserted_rows
In [0]:
%sql
SELECT 
    risk_level,
    COUNT(*) as transactions,
    SUM(isFraud) as actual_fraud,
    ROUND(SUM(isFraud) * 100.0 / COUNT(*), 2) as fraud_rate,
    ROUND(AVG(total_risk_score), 2) as avg_risk_score,
    ROUND(AVG(amount), 2) as avg_amount
FROM clean_transactions
GROUP BY risk_level
ORDER BY fraud_rate DESC;
risk_leveltransactionsactual_fraudfraud_rateavg_risk_scoreavg_amount
CRITICAL_RISK41540584714.0811.55792332.17
HIGH_RISK135544614070.106.41329576.41
MEDIUM_RISK42074259430.023.43124126.02
LOW_RISK75819300.000.8187954.15

Creating Optimized Views for Power BI¶

In [0]:
%sql
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW vw_transactions_powerbi AS
SELECT 
    -- Identifiers
    time_step,
    transaction_type,
    customer_id,
    recipient_id,
    recipient_type,
    
    -- Amount info
    amount,
    amount_category,
    amount_first_digit,
    
    -- Time info
    day_number,
    hour_of_day,
    time_period,
    day_of_week,
    
    -- Balance info
    customer_old_balance,
    customer_new_balance,
    recipient_old_balance,
    recipient_new_balance,
    
    -- Fraud indicators
    isFraud,
    isFlaggedFraud,
    
    -- All flags (for filtering)
    flag_large_transfer,
    flag_no_balance_change,
    flag_balance_mismatch,
    flag_zero_after_txn,
    flag_benford_unusual_digit,
    flag_benford_time,
    flag_benford_pattern,
    flag_benford_amount_anomaly,
    flag_high_velocity,
    flag_unusual_pattern,
    flag_risky_recipient,
    
    -- Risk assessment
    total_risk_score,
    risk_level,
    
    -- Customer context
    total_customer_txns,
    avg_customer_amount,
    unusual_digit_ratio,
    
    -- Recipient context
    recipient_risk_category
    
FROM clean_transactions;
In [0]:
%sql
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW vw_daily_summary_powerbi AS
SELECT 
    day_number,
    day_of_week,
    transaction_type,
    COUNT(*) as transaction_count,
    SUM(amount) as total_amount,
    AVG(amount) as avg_amount,
    SUM(isFraud) as fraud_count,
    ROUND(SUM(isFraud) * 100.0 / COUNT(*), 2) as fraud_rate,
    AVG(total_risk_score) as avg_risk_score,
    SUM(CASE WHEN risk_level = 'CRITICAL_RISK' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as critical_risk_txns,
    SUM(CASE WHEN risk_level = 'HIGH_RISK' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as high_risk_txns
FROM clean_transactions
GROUP BY day_number, day_of_week, transaction_type;

-- 10.3: Customer risk view (for profiling)
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW vw_customer_risk_powerbi AS
SELECT 
    c.customer_id,
    c.total_customer_txns,
    c.avg_customer_amount,
    c.unusual_digit_ratio,
    c.customer_fraud_count,
    ROUND(c.customer_fraud_count * 100.0 / c.total_customer_txns, 2) as customer_fraud_rate,
    COUNT(DISTINCT t.recipient_id) as unique_recipients,
    AVG(t.total_risk_score) as avg_transaction_risk,
    MAX(t.total_risk_score) as max_transaction_risk,
    SUM(CASE WHEN t.risk_level IN ('CRITICAL_RISK', 'HIGH_RISK') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as high_risk_txns
FROM customer_history c
LEFT JOIN clean_transactions t ON c.customer_id = t.customer_id
GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.total_customer_txns, c.avg_customer_amount, 
         c.unusual_digit_ratio, c.customer_fraud_count;

-- 10.4: Benford's Law analysis view (for validation)
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW vw_benford_analysis_powerbi AS
WITH benford_expected AS (
    SELECT 1 as digit, 30.1 as expected_pct UNION ALL
    SELECT 2, 17.6 UNION ALL
    SELECT 3, 12.5 UNION ALL
    SELECT 4, 9.7 UNION ALL
    SELECT 5, 7.9 UNION ALL
    SELECT 6, 6.7 UNION ALL
    SELECT 7, 5.8 UNION ALL
    SELECT 8, 5.1 UNION ALL
    SELECT 9, 4.6
)
SELECT 
    t.amount_first_digit as digit,
    COUNT(*) as actual_count,
    ROUND(COUNT(*) * 100.0 / SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER(), 2) as actual_pct,
    b.expected_pct,
    ROUND(COUNT(*) * 100.0 / SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER() - b.expected_pct, 2) as deviation,
    SUM(CASE WHEN t.isFraud = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as fraud_count,
    ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN t.isFraud = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*), 2) as fraud_rate
FROM clean_transactions t
JOIN benford_expected b ON t.amount_first_digit = b.digit
WHERE t.amount > 1000
GROUP BY t.amount_first_digit, b.expected_pct
ORDER BY t.amount_first_digit;

Final Quality Checks¶

In [0]:
%sql
SELECT 
    'Total Transactions' as metric, 
    CAST(COUNT(*) AS STRING) as value 
FROM clean_transactions
UNION ALL
SELECT 'Total Fraud Cases', CAST(SUM(isFraud) AS STRING)
FROM clean_transactions
UNION ALL
SELECT 'Overall Fraud Rate %', CAST(ROUND(SUM(isFraud) * 100.0 / COUNT(*), 2) AS STRING)
FROM clean_transactions
UNION ALL
SELECT 'Unique Customers', CAST(COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id) AS STRING)
FROM clean_transactions
UNION ALL
SELECT 'Avg Risk Score', CAST(ROUND(AVG(total_risk_score), 2) AS STRING)
FROM clean_transactions;

-- 11.2: Check risk score distribution
SELECT 
    risk_level,
    COUNT(*) as transactions,
    ROUND(COUNT(*) * 100.0 / SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER(), 2) as percentage,
    SUM(isFraud) as fraud_found,
    ROUND(AVG(amount), 2) as avg_amount
FROM clean_transactions
GROUP BY risk_level
ORDER BY 
    CASE risk_level 
        WHEN 'CRITICAL_RISK' THEN 1
        WHEN 'HIGH_RISK' THEN 2
        WHEN 'MEDIUM_RISK' THEN 3
        ELSE 4
    END;

-- 11.3: Validate Benford's flags performance
SELECT 
    flag_benford_unusual_digit,
    COUNT(*) as transactions,
    SUM(isFraud) as fraud,
    ROUND(SUM(isFraud) * 100.0 / COUNT(*), 2) as fraud_rate
FROM clean_transactions
GROUP BY flag_benford_unusual_digit;

-- 11.4: Check time patterns
SELECT 
    time_period,
    COUNT(*) as transactions,
    SUM(isFraud) as fraud,
    ROUND(SUM(isFraud) * 100.0 / COUNT(*), 2) as fraud_rate,
    AVG(total_risk_score) as avg_risk
FROM clean_transactions
GROUP BY time_period
ORDER BY fraud_rate DESC;
time_periodtransactionsfraudfraud_rateavg_risk
Late Night (00-05)11260319941.773.9205527383817484
Morning (06-11)119455720890.173.8569980335806497
Evening (18-23)236566420410.093.7791051476456503
Afternoon (12-17)268978020730.083.7984429953379086
In [0]:
%sql
-- Model Performance Metrics
SELECT 
    -- Detection Rate
    ROUND(SUM(isFraud) * 100.0 / NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN risk_level IN ('CRITICAL_RISK', 'HIGH_RISK') THEN 1 END), 0), 2) as detection_rate,
    
    -- False Positive Rate (high risk but not fraud)
    ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN risk_level IN ('CRITICAL_RISK', 'HIGH_RISK') AND isFraud = 0 THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / 
          NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN risk_level IN ('CRITICAL_RISK', 'HIGH_RISK') THEN 1 END), 0), 2) as false_positive_rate,
    
    -- Risk Score Accuracy
    ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN isFraud = 1 THEN total_risk_score ELSE NULL END), 2) as avg_risk_score_fraud,
    ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN isFraud = 0 THEN total_risk_score ELSE NULL END), 2) as avg_risk_score_normal
FROM clean_transactions;
detection_ratefalse_positive_rateavg_risk_score_fraudavg_risk_score_normal
0.5999.4810.973.8
In [0]:
